name: walkthrough
description: 22-turn debate from docs/example-walkthrough.md encoded as a deterministic event log of a full session (lobby->operate mode change through session-ended). Anna (debater-A) defends modern accredited zoos; Ben (debater-B) defends the negative; Maria (moderator) operates the platform. The fixture exercises decomposition (turns 5, 9), defeater pre-commit (turns 11, 13, 17), per-participant axiom-marks on N12 (turns 14, 16), interpretive split of N14 into N16/N17 (turn 17), annotations A1-A4 (A4 = the turn-17 'shared axiom' note on N12), a capture-node gesture for N18, an edit-wording reword of N15, Anna's A2 reframe as a meta-move with Ben's facet-keyed substance dispute (ADR 0038), a withdrawn classify proposal on N12 (normative -> value; deliberate deviation from the doc's kind=normative to exercise proposal-withdrawn + the value classification), Ben's withdraw-agreement on E3's substance, the E16 defines edge (N1 defines N2), a deadlocked E15 substance round closed by a facet-keyed meta-disagreement mark, facet-keyed votes/commits for every facet-valued proposal (post-ADR-0030 wire shapes; structural proposals stay proposal-keyed), and a snapshot at "Segment 1 close" (turn 22). Walkthrough identifiers map to stable UUIDs: nodes N1-N19 -> 10000010-...-0000000000NN; edges E1-E16 -> 10000020-...-000000000NNN (E11a/b use suffix 011a/011b); annotations A1-A4 -> 10000030-...-00000000000N; snapshot -> 10000040-...-000000000001. Events inserted by the representative-log extension use envelope ids ee000000-...-0000XXXXNNNN (one XXXX block per story insertion); the original events keep their positional ids. Anna's compound opener and Ben's captivity leg are implicit decompose parents (UUIDs ending 0000a0 and 0000b0) that become invisible after the turn-5 and turn-9 decompose commits. E15 is a contradicts edge from N19 (node) to A2 (annotation) — the canonical annotation-endpoint shape unlocked by edge_target_annotation_schema_extension + projection_edge_annotation_endpoint.
